DISCOVERY 610, APPEARANCES: KATHARINE H. PORTER DEPUTY CITY ATTORNEY FOR DEFENDANT AND MOVING PARTY: CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO. PHILIP J. KAPLAN FOR PLAINTIFF AND OPPOSING PARTY: PAUL R. LOZADA (TELEPHONIC APPEARANCE) HEARING HELD AND TENTATIVE ADOPTED WITH MODIFICATIONS. DEFENDANT'S MOTION TO COMPEL COMPLIANCE WITH THE DEPOSITION SUBPOENA IS GRANTED. ANNE COBB IS ORDERED TO COMPLY WITH DEFENDANT'S SUBPOENA AND PRODUCE THE REQUESTED RECORDS BY JANUARY 28, 2005 AND TO APPEAR AND TESTIFY AT DEPOSITION ON JANUARY 31, 2005. PURSUANT TO EVIDENCE CODE SECTION 1016, MS. COBB MAY NOT ASSERT THE PSYCHOTHERAPIST-PATIENT PRIVILEGE FOR EITHER PLAINTIFF OR HIS SPOUSE AS A BASIS FOR WITHHOLDING DOCUMENTS OR TESTIMONY AT DEPOSITION. THE PARTIES ARE ORDERED TO MEET AND CONFER IN ADVANCE OF THE DEPOSITION TO LIMIT THE SCOPE OF THE INQUIRY AT DEPOSITION RELATING TO PLAINTIFF'S SPOUSE TO THOSE QUESTIONS RELATED TO THE CLAIMS ASSERTED BY PLAINTIFF. DEFENDANT'S MOTION TO COMPEL A MENTAL EXAMINATION IS GRANTED. PLAINTIFF IS ORDERED TO ATTEND THE FOLLOWING: 1) JANUARY 27, 2005, COMMENCING AT 10:00 A.M. AND CONTINUING THE REST OF THE BUSINESS DAY, WITH DR. SAUL ROSENBERG, CLINICAL PSYCHOLOGIST, FOR PSYCHIATRIC TESTING, AT 655 REDWOOD HIGHWAY, SUITE 271, MILL VALLEY, CALIFORNIA. (415) 388-8040; 2) JANUARY 28, 2005, COMMENCING AT 10:00 A.M. AND CONTINUING THE REST OF THE BUSINESS DAY, WITH DR. MARK LEVY, LICENSED PSYCHIATRIST, FOR AN ORAL PSYCHIATRIC EXAMINATION AND HISTORY, AT 655 REDWOOD HIGHWAY, SUITE 271, MILL VALLEY, CALIFORNIA. (415) 388-8040; MODIFICATIONS: TO DELETE SCHEDULED TIMES. IME TO BE COMPLETED BY FEBRUARY 14, 2005. PARTIES TO STIPULATE TO EXTENSION OF DISCOVERY CUT OFF FOR LIMITED PURPOSE OF COMPLETING IME. BRUCE E. CHAN.

Mtn For Order Compelling Further Responses Of Deft Ccsf To Special Interoggs (Set 1)
Off Calendar Apr-06-2005 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Philip J. Kaplan for plaintiff and moving party: Paul R. Lozada
David A. Carrillo Deputy City Attorney for defendant and opposing party: City and County of San Francisco.
HEARING HELD and tentative ruling adopted: Motion to compel further responses to interrogatories:
#4, 5, 9: denied. The requested information is protected by the official information privilege.
#8: granted. The objections are overruled and stricken. The Court finds that the interrogatory does not implicate the official information privilege.
#27: denied. The objections are overruled and stricken. The reservation of the right to amend the response is ordered stricken. The response must be full and complete at the time it is made. If there is a need to amend, it must be made in compliance with CCP Section 2030(m). The answer is otherwise sufficient.
#30, 31: granted. The objections are overruled and stricken.
Compliance date of April 21, 2005. COMMISSIONER: BRUCE E. CHAN

Plaintiff Paul R. Lozada'S Motion To File Second Amended Complaint
Off Calendar Dec-21-2004 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Philip J. Kaplan, Esq., Pero & Kaplan (213) 627-5300 for Plaintiff and Moving Party Lozada
David A. Carrillo, Deputy City Attorney (415) 554-4647 for Defendant and Opposing Party City and County of San Francisco
RULING:
A R G U E D; PLAINTIFF PAUL R. LOZADA'S Motion To File Second Amended Complaint is GRANTED; the second amended complaint shall be filed and served no later than 12/28/04 with 30 days leave to answer. Counsel enter the following stipulation on the record: Discovery cutoff is 01/31/05; a motion for summary judgment may be filed and served no later than 02/07/05 with opposition filed and served no later than 02/18/05, reply filed and served no later than 02/23/05 with the motion heard on 02/28/05 in Dept. 301. Counsel further stipulate that it is understood that if the motion for summary judgment cannot be resolved on the merits and cannot be resolved from the bench at the time of the hearing, the trial date may have to be reset. Attorney Kaplan shall submit a proposed form of order. (301/JLW/) Reporter: Christina Paxton, CSR #1558
